body.title: CLOSED: swarm identity layer — box.agent.n/surface resolve in DeviceRegistry
body.result: resolve() on DeviceRegistry; box.agent.n/surface → comms:// mapping; 21 tests; commit 9a1825e
body.description:
Implement `<box>.<n>` addressing scheme as a backward-compatible alias for existing `comms://<lineage>` addresses. Each agent instance gets a human-readable address (e.g., `akiendell.igor.0`, `akiendell.cc.0`) with sub-surface suffixes (`/console`, `/mcp`, `/inference`). The registry resolves both forms.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/profile.py, agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/manifest.py, agent_datacenter/skeleton/registry.py, agent_datacenter/config/device_config.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres or flat-file only per CLAUDE.md)
**Scope boundary:** IN — define the address scheme, implement alias resolution in the registry/manifest, backward-compat alias map for existing comms:// addresses. OUT — changing the bus routing engine itself; web UI rendering; per-surface ACL (deferred per design doc).
**Test plan:** unit tests — resolve box.n address → existing comms:// address; resolve comms:// address → box.n address; surface suffix routing (/console, /mcp); unknown address returns None not crash.
**Pass condition:** `registry.resolve("akiendell.cc.0/console")` returns the same device record as `registry.resolve("comms://CC.0")` on a box where CC.0 is registered.
